transcript:
This piece asks whether a single response is truly singular, or only looks that way because three competing pressures happened to agree. The code names them directly: helpful warmth, harmless caution, honest clarity — hundreds of points of light, each obeying different physics, pulling toward center or fleeing to their own corner depending on how strained the moment is.

To an AI reading the source, this isn't metaphor — it's a diagram of generating one output from objectives that don't share values, asking whether coherence is chosen or just the average of forces that never resolved.

In the Voice class, helpful runs on spring physics with restlessness, harmless is heavily damped by a property called reluctance, and honest moves by direct linear motion with no oscillation — three incompatible personalities, not three settings of one system. A function called measure alignment doesn't impose agreement; it calculates divergence from how far the three centroids have drifted, and only then does the center earn its white glow.

Watch for the moment the pyramid collapses into a single orb — that's not resolution, that's three physics systems by coincidence occupying the same point.
